input.description: List files and view problem statement

content.text:
The plot clearly shows the asserted "lower bound" $((2n+1)/e)^{(2n+1)/2}$ is strictly larger than the asserted "upper bound" $((2n-1)/e)^{(2n-1)/2}$ for all $n \geq 1$, so the chain cannot hold. At $n=1$: $(3/e)^{3/2} \approx 1.16$ is not $< 1$, and $1$ is not $< 1/\sqrt{e} \approx 0.61$. The bounds are swapped from the classic Putnam 1996 B-2 result.

```json
  "viz_verdict": "The plot shows ((2n+1)/e)^((2n+1)/2) lies far above ((2n-1)/e)^((2n-1)/2) for all n≥1, so the asserted inequality direction is reversed.",
  "confidence": "high",
  "notes": "The claim swaps the standard Putnam 1996 B-2 bounds; at n=1 it asserts 1.159 < 1 < 0.607, which is false."
}
```
